changesProposed.name: Engine Ventures

verified.name: Engine Ventures
verified.thesis: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage Tough Tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across deep tech fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing.
verified.notes: Engine Ventures is a separate investment firm that spun out from The Engine (a nonprofit incubator/accelerator founded by MIT in 2016) in 2023. The Engine is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit as of 2025. Source [4] about SpaceX is irrelevant to Engine Ventures and was not used. No AUM, flagship fund name, or current senior partners could be verified from sources. Key partners Katie Rae and Reed Sturtevant are not listed on firm website or Wikipedia.

changesProposed.name: Khosla Ventures

verified.name: Khosla Ventures
verified.thesis: Khosla Ventures is passionate about technology-based economic disruption in many industries. The firm seeks unfair advantages through proprietary and protected technological advances, business model innovations, and unique approaches to markets. The firm believes AI has the power to disrupt numerous economic models and change the way people lead their daily lives.
verified.notes: Keith Rabois returned to firm as managing director in February 2024 (source 4). Most recent AUM figure is $15B as of October 2021 (source 4). No current flagship fund name explicitly stated in sources; Wikipedia mentions 'eighth flagship fund' closed in November 2023 but specific name not provided.

changes.current: Khosla Ventures is passionate about technology-based economic disruption in many industries, seeking unfair advantages through proprietary and protected technological advances, business model innovations, and unique approaches to markets. The firm believes AI has the power to disrupt numerous economic models and change the way we lead our daily lives over the coming years. The firm invests in deep tech where it can be early, bold and impactful.
